records.title: CLI command descriptors own parse and run

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Every command descriptor directly owns executable parse and run functions; dispatch is derived from commandSpecs rather than parserId/runnerId string registries.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { commandSpecs } from "./packages/cli/src/cli/command-spec/index.ts"; const missing=commandSpecs.filter((spec)=>typeof spec.parse!=="function"||typeof spec.run!=="function").map((spec)=>spec.kind); console.log(JSON.stringify({total:commandSpecs.length,executableDescriptors:commandSpecs.length-missing.length,missingCount:missing.length,sample:missing.slice(0,5)})); process.exit(missing.length===0?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: All 100 descriptors still expose string parser/runner identities without executable parse/run ownership.

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Every command spec owns non-empty option descriptions, and reused flag spellings may carry different descriptions in different command contexts.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { commandSpecs } from "./packages/cli/src/cli/command-spec/index.ts"; const malformed=commandSpecs.filter((spec)=>!Array.isArray(spec.options)||spec.options.some((option)=>typeof option.flag!=="string"||typeof option.description!=="string"||option.description.trim()==="")); const descriptions=new Map(); for(const spec of commandSpecs) for(const option of spec.options){const values=descriptions.get(option.flag)??new Set(); values.add(option.description); descriptions.set(option.flag,values);} const contextual=[...descriptions].filter(([,values])=>values.size>1).map(([flag])=>flag); console.log(JSON.stringify({total:commandSpecs.length,malformedCount:malformed.length,contextualFlagCount:contextual.length,contextualSample:contextual.slice(0,5)})); process.exit(malformed.length===0&&contextual.length>0?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: All 100 specs own well-formed option descriptions and six reused flags now have contextual descriptions.

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Every registered API schema is an executable codec and every route resolves its input through that codec; GUI, daemon, preload, and fixtures consume the same transport-neutral contract authority.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { apiSchemaContracts,apiRouteContracts } from "./packages/gui/src/api/api-contract-registry.ts"; const executable=(schema)=>typeof schema.decode==="function"||typeof schema.codec?.decode==="function"; const missing=apiSchemaContracts.filter((schema)=>!executable(schema)).map((schema)=>schema.id); const unresolved=apiRouteContracts.filter((route)=>!apiSchemaContracts.some((schema)=>schema.id===route.inputSchemaId&&executable(schema))).map((route)=>route.id); console.log(JSON.stringify({schemas:apiSchemaContracts.length,executableSchemas:apiSchemaContracts.length-missing.length,routes:apiRouteContracts.length,routesWithExecutableInput:apiRouteContracts.length-unresolved.length,missingSchemaSample:missing.slice(0,5)})); process.exit(missing.length===0&&unresolved.length===0?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: The registry contains 22 schema identifiers but no executable codecs; none of 17 routes has executable input decoding from the authority.

records.title: Daemon command classes derive from command descriptors

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Every CLI command descriptor declares its daemon command class, and the daemon classifier is a derived projection equal to the descriptor set rather than three handwritten action Sets.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { commandSpecs } from "./packages/cli/src/cli/command-spec/index.ts"; import { repoCommandRunClassifiedActionKinds } from "./packages/daemon/src/protocol/method-registry.ts"; const classes=new Set(["repo-read","repo-write","arbiter"]); const missing=commandSpecs.filter((spec)=>!classes.has(spec.daemonClass)).map((spec)=>spec.kind); const derived=commandSpecs.filter((spec)=>classes.has(spec.daemonClass)).map((spec)=>spec.kind).sort(); const mirror=[...repoCommandRunClassifiedActionKinds].sort(); console.log(JSON.stringify({commands:commandSpecs.length,descriptorsWithDaemonClass:derived.length,manualMirrorMembers:mirror.length,setsEqual:JSON.stringify(derived)===JSON.stringify(mirror),missingSample:missing.slice(0,5)})); process.exit(missing.length===0&&JSON.stringify(derived)===JSON.stringify(mirror)?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: All 100 command classes remain in the daemon-side mirror and zero descriptors own the class.

mechanism.mustBeTrue: WriteOp.kind is interpreted once into a complete transaction plan containing validation, touched paths, materialization, and replay metadata; preflight, flush, and recovery consume that plan.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import ts from "typescript"; const file="packages/kernel/src/store/write-journal-operations.ts"; const source=ts.createSourceFile(file,readFileSync(file,"utf8"),ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,true,ts.ScriptKind.TS); const interpreters=[]; const visit=(node,current)=>{let next=current; if(ts.isFunctionDeclaration(node)&&node.name) next=node.name.text; if(ts.isPropertyAccessExpression(node)&&ts.isIdentifier(node.expression)&&node.expression.text==="op"&&node.name.text==="kind"&&next) interpreters.push(next); ts.forEachChild(node,(child)=>visit(child,next));}; visit(source,null); const unique=[...new Set(interpreters)]; console.log(JSON.stringify({opKindInterpreterCount:unique.length,interpreters:unique})); process.exit(unique.length<=1?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: Nine functions currently interpret op.kind, so the transaction meaning is not owned by one plan interface.

records.title: Task-holder mutations are atomic inside the lease module

mechanism.mustBeTrue: The task-holder module itself guards claim and release with a task-scoped cross-process mutation lock; daemon queueing is not a correctness prerequisite.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import ts from "typescript"; const file="packages/kernel/src/local/task-holder-state.ts"; const source=ts.createSourceFile(file,readFileSync(file,"utf8"),ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,true,ts.ScriptKind.TS); let helper=false,lockCalls=0; const visit=(node)=>{if(ts.isFunctionDeclaration(node)&&node.name?.text==="withTaskHolderMutationLock") helper=true; if(ts.isCallExpression(node)&&ts.isIdentifier(node.expression)&&node.expression.text==="withTaskHolderMutationLock") lockCalls++; ts.forEachChild(node,visit);}; visit(source); console.log(JSON.stringify({atomicMutationHelper:helper,guardedMutationCallsites:lockCalls})); process.exit(helper&&lockCalls>=2?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: The module owns an atomic mutation helper and both claim/release mutation paths call it.

records.title: Daemon process runtime belongs to the daemon package

mechanism.mustBeTrue: packages/daemon exports and owns single- and multi-repository daemon runtime lifecycle; kernel/store exposes only repository write-session capabilities and does not own process hosting.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import * as daemon from "./packages/daemon/src/index.ts"; import * as store from "./packages/kernel/src/store/index.ts"; const result={daemonOwnsRuntime:typeof daemon.createDaemonRuntime==="function"&&typeof daemon.createMultiRepoDaemonRuntime==="function",kernelStoreOwnsRuntime:typeof store.createDaemonRuntime==="function"||typeof store.createMultiRepoDaemonRuntime==="function"}; console.log(JSON.stringify(result)); process.exit(result.daemonOwnsRuntime&&!result.kernelStoreOwnsRuntime?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: The daemon package does not export the runtime while kernel/store still does.

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Exactly one extensions module owns spawnSync and sandbox permission/error/diff/evidence behavior; script host and preset runner are thin context adapters over it.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync,readdirSync } from "node:fs"; import path from "node:path"; const dir="packages/cli/src/commands/extensions"; const owners=readdirSync(dir).filter((name)=>name.endsWith(".ts")).filter((name)=>readFileSync(path.join(dir,name),"utf8").includes("spawnSync")); console.log(JSON.stringify({spawnSyncOwnerCount:owners.length,owners})); process.exit(owners.length===1&&owners[0]==="script-executor.ts"?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: script-executor.ts is the sole extensions module that owns spawnSync.

records.title: CLI core does not know preset IDs

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Preset policy and template privileges are interpreted through a generic declaration envelope; extension core contains no exact preset-ID dispatch literals.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import ts from "typescript"; const ids=new Set(["create-milestone","milestone-closeout","decision-conformance"]); const files=["packages/cli/src/commands/extensions/preset-policy.ts","packages/cli/src/commands/extensions/state.ts"]; const hits=[]; for(const file of files){const source=ts.createSourceFile(file,readFileSync(file,"utf8"),ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,true,ts.ScriptKind.TS); const visit=(node)=>{if(ts.isStringLiteral(node)&&ids.has(node.text)) hits.push({file,value:node.text,line:source.getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(node.getStart()).line+1}); ts.forEachChild(node,visit);}; visit(source);} console.log(JSON.stringify({presetIdLiteralHits:hits.length,hits})); process.exit(hits.length===0?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: The two extension-core files contain 13 exact literals for three preset IDs.

records.title: Relation authored sources have one catalog

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Relation graph collection and projection freshness consume one authored-source catalog; source-kind literals are not independently redeclared in both consumers.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import ts from "typescript"; const domain=new Set(["task-index","task-facts","decision-document"]); const values=(file)=>{const source=ts.createSourceFile(file,readFileSync(file,"utf8"),ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,true,ts.ScriptKind.TS); const found=new Set(); const visit=(node)=>{if(ts.isStringLiteral(node)&&domain.has(node.text)) found.add(node.text); ts.forEachChild(node,visit);}; visit(source); return found;}; const graph=values("packages/kernel/src/projection/relation-graph-projection.ts"); const hash=values("packages/kernel/src/projection/sqlite-task-source.ts"); const duplicated=[...graph].filter((value)=>hash.has(value)).sort(); console.log(JSON.stringify({graphKinds:[...graph].sort(),hashKinds:[...hash].sort(),manuallyDuplicatedKinds:duplicated})); process.exit(duplicated.length===0?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: All three relation authored-source kinds are still independently present in both consumers.

records.title: Code-doc reconciliation uses the canonical VCS port

mechanism.mustBeTrue: VersionControlSystem owns the path-at-ref query needed by code-doc reconciliation, and application code does not declare a second GitRunner seam.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import ts from "typescript"; const members=(file,name)=>{const source=ts.createSourceFile(file,readFileSync(file,"utf8"),ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,true,ts.ScriptKind.TS); const found=[]; for(const statement of source.statements) if(ts.isInterfaceDeclaration(statement)&&statement.name.text===name) for(const member of statement.members) if(member.name&&ts.isIdentifier(member.name)) found.push(member.name.text); return found;}; const vcs=members("packages/kernel/src/ports/version-control-system.ts","VersionControlSystem"); const app=members("packages/application/src/code-doc-reconciliation.ts","GitRunner"); const pathAtRef=vcs.includes("pathExistsAtCommit")||vcs.includes("pathExistsAtRef"); console.log(JSON.stringify({vcsSupportsPathAtRef:pathAtRef,applicationGitRunnerMembers:app})); process.exit(pathAtRef&&app.length===0?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: The canonical VCS port lacks path-at-ref support while application still declares the two-method GitRunner seam.

records.title: Deterministic checks execute before PR admission

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Every non-aggregate gate declared in check:pr and executed by main full-check has at least one pull-request workflow job before merge.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; const manifest=JSON.parse(readFileSync("tools/gate-manifest.json","utf8")); const gaps=manifest.gates.filter((gate)=>gate.aggregate!==true&&gate.executionSurfaces?.packageJson?.checkPr===true&&(gate.executionSurfaces?.rewriteCi?.pullRequestJobs?.length??0)===0&&(gate.executionSurfaces?.rewriteCi?.nonPullRequestJobs??[]).includes("full-check")).map((gate)=>gate.id); console.log(JSON.stringify({prDeclaredButNotRunBeforeMerge:gaps.length,gates:gaps})); process.exit(gaps.length===0?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: Eleven non-aggregate checks are declared in check:pr but have no pull-request workflow job and first run in full-check.

records.title: Task write-route policy is declared once

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Each Task write route declares its command class in the route authority, including tasks.review as arbiter; daemon enforcement and governance projections derive from that declaration.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { apiRouteContracts } from "./packages/gui/src/api/api-contract-registry.ts"; const writes=apiRouteContracts.filter((route)=>route.id.startsWith("tasks.")&&!["GET","WS"].includes(route.method)); const missing=writes.filter((route)=>!["repo-write","arbiter"].includes(route.commandClass)).map((route)=>route.id); const review=writes.find((route)=>route.id==="tasks.review"); console.log(JSON.stringify({taskWriteRoutes:writes.length,declarativeCommandClasses:writes.length-missing.length,missing,reviewClass:review?.commandClass??null})); process.exit(missing.length===0&&review?.commandClass==="arbiter"?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: All three Task write routes lack a declarative command class and tasks.review has no authority-owned arbiter classification.

records.title: Renderer Task server state has one owner

mechanism.mustBeTrue: TanStack Query is the sole owner of Task server state; optimistic overlay, rollback, refetch, and domain failure handling live in the query/mutation module rather than a second App useState mirror.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import ts from "typescript"; const file="packages/gui/src/renderer/App.tsx"; const source=ts.createSourceFile(file,readFileSync(file,"utf8"),ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,true,ts.ScriptKind.TSX); let queryOwner=false,localTaskOwners=0; const visit=(node)=>{if(ts.isCallExpression(node)&&ts.isIdentifier(node.expression)&&node.expression.text==="useTasksQuery") queryOwner=true; if(ts.isVariableDeclaration(node)&&ts.isArrayBindingPattern(node.name)&&node.name.elements[0]&&ts.isBindingElement(node.name.elements[0])&&ts.isIdentifier(node.name.elements[0].name)&&node.name.elements[0].name.text==="tasks"&&node.initializer&&ts.isCallExpression(node.initializer)&&ts.isIdentifier(node.initializer.expression)&&node.initializer.expression.text==="useState") localTaskOwners++; ts.forEachChild(node,visit);}; visit(source); const owners=(queryOwner?1:0)+localTaskOwners; console.log(JSON.stringify({queryOwner,localTaskOwners,totalServerStateOwners:owners})); process.exit(queryOwner&&owners===1?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: App owns a mutable Task useState mirror in addition to the query owner.

mechanism.mustBeTrue: The kernel projection exposes the complete Decision/Task/fact vocabulary and application consumes those types directly; application does not redeclare projection-row interfaces or widen domain unions to strings.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import ts from "typescript"; const names=(file)=>{const source=ts.createSourceFile(file,readFileSync(file,"utf8"),ts.ScriptTarget.Latest,true,ts.ScriptKind.TS); return new Set(source.statements.filter(ts.isInterfaceDeclaration).map((node)=>node.name.text));}; const kernel=names("packages/kernel/src/projection/types.ts"); const application=names("packages/application/src/index.ts"); const duplicated=[...kernel].filter((name)=>application.has(name)).sort(); console.log(JSON.stringify({duplicatedProjectionInterfaces:duplicated.length,names:duplicated})); process.exit(duplicated.length===0?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: DecisionProjectionRow is still independently declared in kernel projection and application. This detector covers the authority duplication; renderer-invented defaults remain supporting evidence for the same open mechanism.

records.title: Physical-I/O debt is narrow, owned, expiring, and bypass-tested

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Every physical-I/O exception is narrower than a whole file and carries owner plus expiry; the boundary gate requires documented bypass fixtures.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import { portPhysicalIoBoundaryKnownDebt as debt } from "./tools/port-physical-io-boundary-known-debt.mjs"; const manifest=JSON.parse(readFileSync("tools/gate-manifest.json","utf8")); const lint=manifest.gates.find((gate)=>gate.id==="lint"); const governed=debt.filter((entry)=>typeof entry.owner==="string"&&entry.owner&&typeof entry.expiresAt==="string"&&entry.expiresAt&&(Array.isArray(entry.symbols)||Array.isArray(entry.imports))); console.log(JSON.stringify({debtEntries:debt.length,entriesWithOwnerExpiryAndNarrowScope:governed.length,bypassFixtureRequired:lint?.bypassFixtureRequired??null})); process.exit(governed.length===debt.length&&lint?.bypassFixtureRequired===true?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: All 22 exceptions are file-level entries without owner/expiry/narrow scope, and the manifest does not require bypass fixtures.

records.title: Metadata-only PR fast paths prove the same source SHA

mechanism.mustBeTrue: Every Mergify metadata-only fast-path acceptance step consumes the PR head SHA and executes a verifier proving that the same source SHA already completed source checks; an echo-only green path is not proof.

detection.command: node --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; const lines=readFileSync(".github/workflows/rewrite-ci.yml","utf8").split(/\r?\n/u); const starts=lines.map((line,index)=>line.trim()==="- name: Accept Mergify queue metadata edit"?index:-1).filter((index)=>index>=0); const blocks=starts.map((start)=>{let end=start+1; while(end<lines.length&&!(lines[end].startsWith("      - "))) end++; return lines.slice(start,end).join("\n");}); const verified=blocks.filter((block)=>(block.includes("PR_HEAD_SHA")||block.includes("github.event.pull_request.head.sha"))&&block.includes("run:")&&!block.includes("run: echo")); console.log(JSON.stringify({metadataFastPathSteps:blocks.length,stepsWithSameShaVerification:verified.length,unverifiedSteps:blocks.length-verified.length})); process.exit(blocks.length>0&&verified.length===blocks.length?0:1);'

lastRun.head: da5c205837740cfa3f9078e0a851605073a08af4
lastRun.interpretation: All nine metadata-only acceptance steps are echo-only paths with no same-SHA verifier.
